reviewer.evidencePolicy: Only mark evidenceStatus=satisfied for the three CLI reference claims covered by focused tests, live CLI packets, and read-only subagent boundary review.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic doctor adapter selection and CLI guidance behavior.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused doctor tests showing plain doctor does not guess a named adapter, CLI help preserving --adapter-name guidance, and a live `doctor --adapter-name self-dogfood-eval` run that validates the explicit named adapter.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep named-adapter doctor guidance and explicit adapter validation separate from any future auto-selection behavior.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names claim-discovery inventory and proof routing behavior before any eval fixture authoring.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused claim-discover tests and a live tiny-repo `claim discover` run that emitted a cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1 packet with human-auditable, deterministic, and cautilus-eval proof routes without writing eval fixtures.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep claim discover as proof planning and inventory; fixture writing remains host-owned or handled by later eval-planning steps.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names a deterministic CLI guard that prevents refresh-plan packets from overwriting saved claim-state paths.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by the refresh-plan overwrite regression test: `claim discover --previous <state> --refresh-plan --output <state>` exits 1, reports the saved-state overwrite guard, and leaves the existing cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1 packet intact.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep refresh-plan outputs separate from configured saved claim-state paths.
